column1: 2am advisory is out. Up to 80% chance wave becomes a depression or a tropical storm. Red hatched area includes the West coast of Florida and extends into the Gulf. Rainy and squally weather along the coast late Saturday and into Sunday. Might get a track on Friday if NHC decides to pull the trigger on a PTC advisory.  (Potential Tropical Cyclone).

More east = less time over the gulf and with close proximity to the peninsula will limit strengthening. It’s been constantly trending east all day, and it wouldn’t surprise me to continue to trend even more east. These storms almost always trend east and ultimately go more east in the end. This isn’t “the storm”. A depression, maybe a low end trop storm. If it were more west it’d no doubt be stronger and have more time over the gulf. So a continued trend east is not a bad thing for Tampa bay

hello from across the pond! I’m a FL native but I am by no means an expert. But what I can say is (TYPICALLY) they only last 1-3 days. The problem would be if it hit really hard there could be some flooding that takes a while to drain. I wouldn’t worry too much about it, by the time the 14th is here it’ll have already gone through and things should hopefully be back to normal for the most part. Also Orlando is pretty far inland, about 2-3 hours (100+ miles) from the coast so flooding isn’t really an issue there normally. By the time the storm hits Orlando it’s usually pretty weak.
